milestoneTitle: Phase M9.18b.2 — Revert glow cascade + fix .dash-tile activation-elevation parity. User flagged the M9.18a.2 blur-gradient cascade as risky: unifying box-shadow across disparate components can shift compositor layers / stacking contexts / backdrop-filter interactions in ways that are hard to predict. Reverted: .panel-tile/.continue-row:hover, .tool-modal-learn-body focus, .dash-tile-icon delete armed, .learn-action-btn-danger armed, .learn-zone-drop-candidate/-active, --glow-focus token, CLAUDE.md 'soft glows' bullet, design-review SKILL.md Rule 11. Kept: .dash-tile:hover blur-gradient (M9.18a.1, the originally-complained-about glow cutoff on the new dash tile component — stays fixed per-component). New fix: added .dash-tile to the .dash-panel.is-activated / .learn-zone.is-activated child selector at style.css:2044-2052 so migrated Learn + Projects tiles visually lift on scroll activation the same way .continue-row / .panel-tile still do; that's the real reason activation looked broken — Tools tiles (still .continue-row) lifted while Learn + Projects tiles (now .dash-tile) stayed flat. Memory: feedback_unify_with_overrides.md saved — prefer unified classes with opt-in overrides, not sweeping cascades.
